105.received:
1. Open Chrome or Safari
2. Navigate to https://www.jumpkit.app
3. Chrome: confirm the padlock icon appears in the address bar (left side). Safari: confirm the URL shows https:// with no warning - click the page settings icon (left of URL) and verify "Connection is encrypted". Note: Safari 15+ removed the explicit padlock; a clean address bar with https:// is the equivalent.
4. Navigate to http://jumpkit.app - confirm the address bar changes to https:// (redirect working)
5. Mark Pass if both steps succeed with no SSL warnings

123.received: No backup notification found after runCloudBackup() - notification may not have been created.
123.message: No backup notification found after runCloudBackup() - notification may not have been created.

124.received: Click the user menu (top-right) → click Logout → verify you are redirected to the login screen and cannot navigate back without logging in again.

125.received:
1. Open Supabase dashboard for your JumpKit project.
2. Go to Project Settings → Database → Backups.
3. Confirm the backup plan is active (Pro plan = daily backups; Free plan = no auto-backups).
4. If on free plan, consider upgrading to Pro or scheduling manual exports.
5. Mark as Pass once confirmed.

126.received:
1. Create a separate Supabase project for development (free tier is fine).
2. Add DEV_SUPABASE_URL to your supabase/config.js pointing to the dev project.
3. Re-run this test - it will pass automatically once a separate URL is configured.

128.received:
Step 1: Go to the Jumps page → click any jump with a URL (e.g. www.google.com) → verify it opens in your browser.
Step 2: Click any jump with a local directory path → verify it opens in your file explorer.

received.text: Open the LS checkout page - confirm Test mode is active by verifying the orange "Test mode" banner at the top of the page.
Complete a LS test checkout using a fresh test email that has NO JumpKit account - e.g. a Gmail + alias like jeffroder+testpending@gmail.com. Use test card 4242 4242 4242 4242.
Wait ~5 seconds, then confirm the pending_upgrades row was created by the webhook.
Go to the JumpKit sign-up page and create an account with that same test email.
Sign in with that email - apply-pending-upgrade fires automatically on login. Confirm the profile was upgraded.
received.cmd: SELECT subscription_tier, subscription_status FROM profiles WHERE email='jeffroder+testpending@gmail.com';

received.text: Clean up - delete the test profile and any leftover pending row.
received.cmd:
DELETE FROM profiles WHERE email='jeffroder+testpending@gmail.com';
DELETE FROM pending_upgrades WHERE email='jeffroder+testpending@gmail.com';

148.received:
1. Keep this device logged in.
2. Open a second JumpKit instance on the same Mac using one of the Terminal commands above (copy button available).
3. In the second instance, enter the same email + password and click Sign In.
4. Verify the "Already Logged In" modal appears showing device and last-seen time.
5. Click Cancel - verify you are NOT redirected to app.html.
6. Click Sign In again and this time click "Log out other device & continue" - verify you are redirected to app.html on the second instance.
7. After testing, click the Cleanup command above to remove the temp profile.

149.received:
1. Keep first device open and active on any page.
2. On second device, perform force-logout (test 148 step 6).
3. On first device, wait up to 30 seconds.
4. Verify the red warning toast appears and the app redirects to login.

155.received:
1. On this instance (device A): Settings → Maintenance → Export. Save the JSON file.
2. Open a second JumpKit instance on the same Mac using one of the Terminal commands above (copy button available) - this is your simulated device B. ⚠️ Log in with a DIFFERENT account on the second instance (using the same account will trigger the double-login conflict modal - see test 148).
3. Log in on the second instance with a different account.
4. On device B: Settings → Maintenance → Import. Select the exported JSON file.
5. Verify the import summary shows the expected count of imported jumps.
6. Navigate to Jumps page and confirm all personal columns and jumps appear.
7. Run Import again with the same file - verify all jumps are skipped (0 imported) with no duplicates created.
8. After testing, click the Cleanup command above to remove the temp profile.

161.received:
1. Go to Jumps page → click "Configure Columns".
2. Click "+ Add Column" at the bottom - a new blank row should appear.
3. Enter a column name and toggle it visible.
4. Click Save Columns. Verify the new column appears in the jump view.
5. Reopen Configure Columns. Find the new row and click its trash icon.
6. An inline confirmation row should appear ("Delete X and all N jumps?"). Click "Yes, Delete".
7. Click Save Columns. Verify the column is gone.
8. If shared/team columns are present, verify their trash icon is greyed out and non-clickable with tooltip "Team columns can only be hidden, not removed".

received.text: Install the test installer (.exe) and confirm the NSIS installer completes without errors and the app launches.
Confirm the SQLite database is created at %APPDATA%\JumpKit\jumpkit.db (not a temp or app-bundle path).
Confirm auto-backup writes to the user-selected folder on Windows (no path separator errors).
Confirm jump URLs open correctly in the default Windows browser via shell.openExternal().
Confirm the folder-picker (deployment folder, file dialogs) works on Windows paths including spaces and Unicode characters.
Confirm the Windows build is code-signed: right-click the .exe → Properties → Digital Signatures tab → should show a valid signature.
Confirm the auto-updater can see the latest release: check that latest.yml is present in the GitHub release assets and the app detects the update banner within 30 seconds of launch.
